title: Set Up and Manage Google Cloud Service Account Access

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
Some Atlas features such as [federated database instances](atlas-data-federation) require access to resources in your Google Cloud Storage
environment. To grant access in a secure manner, create or
update a Google Cloud [Service Account](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-account-overview)
with access policy.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To configure Google Cloud Service Account access, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)
access to the project.

Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves to the
project as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
- An Atlas account.

- An Atlas `M10+` cluster hosted on Google Cloud.

sections.heading: Set Up Google Cloud Service Account Access
sections.body:
You can set up a Google Cloud service account with access policy for your
Atlas project from the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.**Ui:**Atlas UIYou can create a Google Cloud Service Account entirely from within
the Atlas Data Federation UI.

sections.heading: Step 1: Configure Google Cloud Service Account access.
sections.body:
Click **Configure** for Google Cloud.

Atlas displays the **Authorize Google Cloud
Service Account** page the first time. Subsequently,
Atlas displays the **Google Cloud Service
Account Access** page.

sections.heading: Step 2: Create a Google Cloud Service Account.
sections.body:
- Click **Create Google Cloud Service Account**.

- In the modal window that appears, click
  **Create**.

- Click **Done** to finalize creation of your
  Google Cloud Service Account.**Api:**APITo create an Google Cloud service account with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send `POST` requests to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access) endpoint first with the ID of the
project for which you wish to create a service account and then
with the ID of the project and service account for which you
set up access. To learn more about the request path and body
parameters, see [Create One Cloud Provider Access
Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcloudprovideraccess) and
[Authorize One Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-authorizecloudprovideraccessrole).

sections.heading: View Authorized Google Cloud Service Accounts
sections.body:
You can view all Google Cloud service accounts for your Atlas project
from the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized Google Cloud service accounts.
sections.body:
The **Google Cloud Service Account Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of service accounts
configured for your project. For each service account, the
page displays the following:| Field Name      | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Service Account | The ID of the service account.                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Created Date    | The date when you created and associated the service account with Atlas.                                                                                                                                                             |
| Actions         | The actions that you can take on the associated service account. You can do the following:Click to remove the service account authorization for Atlas.Click to view service account details or create a federated database instance. |**Api:**APITo view all the Google Cloud service accounts with access policy
for your Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET`
request to the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access) endpoint with the ID of the
project. To learn more about the syntax and parameters to
include in the request, see [Return All Cloud
Provider Access Roles](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listcloudprovideraccessroles).

sections.heading: View Authorized Google Cloud Service Account Details
sections.body:
You can view the details of an authorized Google Cloud Service Account from
the Atlas Administration API or Atlas UI.**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized Google Cloud service accounts.
sections.body:
The **Google Cloud Service Account Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of service accounts
configured for your project.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the ellipsis () for the Service Account and select  from the dropdown.
sections.body:
Atlas displays the **Service Account
Details** window. You can view and copy the following
information about the authorized Service Account:

| Field Name                | Description                                   |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Atlas GCP Service Account | The ID of the Atlas Google Cloud application. |
| Service Account ID        | The ID of the service account.                |

sections.heading: Step 3: Click  to close the window.
sections.body:
**Api:**APITo view a Google Cloud service account with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET` request to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access) endpoint with the ID of the
project. To learn more about the syntax and parameters to
include in the request, see [Return specified
Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getcloudprovideraccessrole).

sections.heading: Remove Authorized Google Cloud Service Account
sections.body:
You can't remove a Service Account that is currently in use. You can
remove unused Google Cloud Service Accounts from the Atlas Administration API
or Atlas UI.**Ui:**Atlas UI

sections.heading: Step 1: View the authorized Google Cloud service accounts.
sections.body:
The **Google Cloud Service Account Access** page
displays. The page displays the list of services accounts
configured for your project.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the  for the Service Account that you wish to remove.
Step 3: Enter the ID of the Service Account that is shown in the window and click  to remove it.
sections.body:
**Api:**APITo remove an Google Cloud service account with access policy for your
Atlas project through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `DELETE` request to
the [cloudProviderAccess](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-cloud-provider-access)
endpoint with the ID of the project, the name of the cloud
provider, and the ID of the service account that you wish to
remove. To learn more about the syntax and parameters to include
in the request, see
[Deauthorize One Cloud Provider Access Role](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deauthorizecloudprovideraccessrole).
